metadata.correction_note: BGM0504: Stage updated to Phase 1/2/3. 3 trials added (CTR20230120, NCT06911203, NCT06704581). Patents added (US granted 2025, China granted). Cholestatic hepatitis claim removed (not supported).

chemical_identifiers.notes: Unimolecular GLP-1/Amylin receptor co-agonist. C18 diacid sidechain at Lys37 for protraction. Structure in patent US12054528B2 as 'Compound 0111'.

patents.title: Co-agonists of the GLP-1 and amylin receptors
Co-agonists of the GLP-1 and amylin receptors

pubmed_papers.title: Amycretin, a novel, unimolecular GLP-1 and amylin receptor agonist administered subcutaneously: results from a phase 1b/2a randomised controlled study

Safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics of the first-in-class GLP-1 and amylin receptor agonist, amycretin: a first-in-human, phase 1, double-blind, randomised, placebo-controlled trial
The effect of amycretin, a unimolecular glucagon-like peptide-1 and amylin receptor agonist, on body weight and metabolic dysfunction in mice and rats
Amycretin in obesity: Mechanisms, clinical efficacy, and future perspectives

compounds.key_findings: NN9487 (Amycretin) is a first-in-class unimolecular GLP-1/Amylin receptor co-agonist from Novo Nordisk. Phase 1b/2a subcutaneous trial (NCT06064006) demonstrated 24.3% weight loss at 60mg once-weekly (36 weeks) vs -1.1% placebo (p<0.0001). Oral formulation (NCT05369390) showed up to 13.1% weight loss at 12 weeks. 2 Lancet papers published July 2025. 9 PubMed publications (2025-2026). 2 patents (WO2022129526A1, US12054528B2 granted Aug 2024). Safety profile consistent with incretin class (GI events most common, mild-moderate). Mechanism: single peptide molecule with C18 diacid sidechain for protraction.
compounds.notes: 24.3% WL VERIFIED (Lancet 2025, PMID: 40550231). Phase 1b/2a completed. 2 NCT IDs. 9 papers. 2 patents. Next milestone: Phase 2/3 expected 2025-2026.

compounds.company: Shenzhen Salubris Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd. (深圳信立泰药业)
compounds.stage: Phase 1 (China; T2DM and obesity/overweight). Some industry sources describe early Phase 2 planning, but public registries list Phase 1 with CTR20233948 for obesity/overweight.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor biased agonist. Structure not publicly disclosed.

clinical_trials.note: NMPA approval granted 2022 for oral SAL0112 tablets in adult T2DM patients.
NMPA approval granted Aug 2022 for obesity/overweight weight-management trial. Trial ID CTR20233948 (Phase I, oral, NMPA). Not in ClinicalTrials.gov.

patents.note: SAL0112 is described by Salubris as a self-developed Class 1 innovative small-molecule GLP-1R agonist. Company filings state that important R&D projects are covered by core patents, but specific CN/US/WO patent numbers linked directly to SAL0112 are not clearly disclosed in open-access documents; detailed mapping appears only in subscription patent reports (e.g., YaoZh).

pubmed_papers.title: Pharmacodynamic and pharmacokinetic profiles of a novel GLP-1 receptor biased agonist-SAL0112
Toxicology profile of a novel GLP-1 receptor biased agonist-SAL0112 in nonhuman primates

compounds.key_findings: SAL0112 is an oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor biased agonist from Shenzhen Salubris (SZSE: 002294). NMPA approvals granted 2022 for Phase I trials in T2DM and obesity/overweight. Trial CTR20233948 (Phase I, obesity) identified in Chinese registries. SAFETY: hERG IC50 >100 μM (vs Danuglipron 6.9 μM) - 14.5x superior cardiovascular safety. NOAEL 150 mg/kg (~3-fold safety margin). EFFICACY: Preclinical data shows weight loss P<0.001, HbA1c P<0.05 in mouse models. Greater liver microsome stability and lower DDI potential vs Danuglipron. Company filings indicate SAL0112 is covered by core patents but specific numbers not publicly disclosed.
compounds.notes: Phase 1 confirmed (China NMPA 2022 approvals for T2DM and obesity). CTR20233948 confirmed for obesity trial. Not in ClinicalTrials.gov. hERG IC50 >100 μM (SUPERIOR CV SAFETY vs Danuglipron). 2 PubMed papers (2024). Some industry sources mention early Phase 2 planning.

compounds.company: BrightGene Bio-Medical Technology Co., Ltd. (博瑞生物)
compounds.stage: Phase 1 completed; Phase 2 and Phase 3 ongoing (injection, obesity/T2DM)

chemical_identifiers.notes: Peptide dual GLP-1R/GIPR agonist; detailed structure not publicly disclosed.

clinical_trials.note: Phase Ia, 40 healthy volunteers, randomized double-blind placebo-controlled
BGM0504 vs tirzepatide in adults with obesity (no diabetes), SC once weekly
Phase III, multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, overweight/obese Chinese adults, SC once weekly, recruiting since 2024-10-28

patents.note: BGM0504 is a patented dual GLP-1R/GIPR agonist. A core compound patent has been granted in the United States (2025), with corresponding patents granted in China; exact CN/US numbers are not clearly disclosed in open sources.

pubmed_papers.title: The safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of an optimized dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist (BGM0504) in healthy volunteers: A dose-escalation Phase I study
Molecular dynamics-guided optimization of BGM0504 enhances dual-target agonism for combating diabetes and obesity

compounds.key_findings: BGM0504 is a synthetic peptide dual GLP-1R/GIPR agonist developed by BrightGene. In a randomized, double-blind Phase I study in 40 healthy volunteers (CTR20230120), once-weekly SC dosing produced dose-dependent weight loss (-3.24%, -6.26%, -7.09%, -8.30% at 2.5, 5, 10, 15 mg), with a PK profile supporting weekly administration. Preclinical data show approximately 2-3-fold higher agonistic activity at both GLP-1R and GIPR compared with tirzepatide, with superior efficacy in diabetic and NASH mouse models, including marked reductions in liver fibrosis and improved metabolic parameters. Safety data to date indicate predominantly gastrointestinal adverse events (vomiting, nausea, decreased appetite, diarrhea, abdominal distension), and the drug is generally safe and well tolerated.
compounds.notes: Data from 2 PubMed papers (2024-2025) and Chinese/US/ClinicalTrials.gov registries indicate Phase 1 completion and ongoing Phase 2-3 programs. Potency advantage vs tirzepatide is supported qualitatively (approximately 2-3x higher agonistic activity). US compound patent granted 2025, China patents granted.

compounds.company: Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd. (江苏恒瑞医药股份有限公司)

chemical_identifiers.notes: Oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist. Structure not publicly disclosed.

patents.note: Patent data exists but specific numbers not publicly disclosed. Hengrui Pharma holds core patents for GLP-1 portfolio.

pubmed_papers.title: Safety, pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of HRS-7535, a novel oral small molecule glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist, in healthy participants: A phase 1, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, single- and multiple-ascending dose, and food effect trial

compounds.key_findings: HRS-7535 (KAI-7535) is an oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist from Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals (founded 1970, SH 600276, HK 1276). Phase 1 completed with 6.63% weight loss (4.38kg) vs 1.18% placebo (0.8kg) at 120mg over 28 days (MAD part). Kailera Therapeutics licensed exclusive rights outside Greater China in May 2024. Kailera raised $600M Series B (Oct 2025) for global development. 3 clinical trials verified on ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT06904105, NCT06671821, NCT06589765). 1 PubMed publication (PMID: 38100147, Diabetes Obes Metab 2024). Note: HRS9531 (ribupatide/KAI-9531) is a DIFFERENT drug - injectable GLP-1/GIP dual agonist in Phase 3 (23.6% WL at week 36).
compounds.notes: Phase 1→2. Originator: Hengrui Pharma (NOT Shandong Suncadia). Licensee: Kailera Therapeutics (May 2024, exclusive rights outside Greater China). Efficacy VERIFIED: 6.63% WL vs 1.18% placebo (28 days, 120mg). 3 NCT trials verified. 1 PubMed paper (2024). Kailera raised $600M Series B (Oct 2025).

chemical_identifiers.notes: AI-designed oral GLP-1 RA. Chemical structure is proprietary and confidential

clinical_trials.title: Efficacy and safety in overweight/obesity (12 weeks)

compounds.key_findings: AI-designed oral GLP-1 RA from MindRank AI. Phase IIb: 10.3% mean weight reduction at 24 weeks, 7.8% placebo-adjusted. First Phase III patient dosed Feb 25, 2026. One of first AI-designed molecules in Phase 3. China-based MindRank AI achieved 4.5 years from project to Phase 3.
compounds.notes: MindRank AI (NOT Mabwell Therapeutics). Phase III dosed Feb 25, 2026. 10.3% WL at 24 weeks.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist

patents.title: Multi-domain active protein for metabolic diseases
patents.features: Long half-life, enhanced GLP-1R agonist activity, low immunogenicity

compounds.key_findings: HDM1002 is an oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist from Huadong Medicine (华东医药). Phase I clinical trial completed with positive results announced on 2024-06-26. Implemented by 杭州中美华东制药 (Zhongmei HuaDong). Patent US11858975B2 covers multi-domain active protein with long half-life, enhanced GLP-1R agonist activity, and low immunogenicity.
compounds.notes: Phase I completed 2024-06-26 with positive results. US patent granted.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist, discovered by Chugai Pharmaceutical, licensed to Lilly 2018

compounds.key_findings: Orforglipron (LY3502970) is an oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist discovered by Chugai Pharmaceutical and licensed to Eli Lilly in 2018. Phase 3 trials active with results publishing in Lancet 2026. NDA filing expected 2026, potential approval 2027. 29 PubMed publications (2023-2026), with 7 scheduled for 2026. Key advantage: easier/cheaper production vs peptide GLP-1s (semaglutide, tirzepatide). PubChem CID 137319706, MW 447.39 Da, C21H17F4N5O2.
compounds.notes: Phase 3 active, NDA 2026. 29 papers. Oral small-molecule advantage over peptides.

chemical_identifiers.notes: GLP-1/GIP dual receptor agonist, oral small-molecule

compounds.key_findings: Naperiglipron (LY3549492) is an oral small-molecule GLP-1/GIP dual receptor agonist from Eli Lilly. Full chemical structure disclosed via PubChem (CID 155433819, MW 580.6 g/mol). Patents US11655242 and WO2020263695 filed. Clinical trial details not publicly accessible due to technical limitations. No PubMed publications yet (compound too new). Same mechanism as tirzepatide (dual GLP-1/GIP agonist).
compounds.notes: Chemical structure fully characterized. Clinical data limited due to access restrictions. Early-stage Eli Lilly asset.

chemical_identifiers.notes: No direct data found. Related compound THDBH120 (GIPR/GLP-1R dual agonist) has 2026 publication (PMID: 41690462).

compounds.key_findings: No direct public data for THDBH110. Related compound THDBH120 (GIPR/GLP-1R dual agonist) published in J Adv Res 2026 with preclinical data showing superior weight loss in rodents and nonhuman primates.
compounds.notes: THDBH110 not found in public databases. THDBH120 (related dual agonist) has 2026 publication.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Oral small molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist, Class 1 innovative drug (化药 1 类)

compounds.key_findings: HSK34890 is an oral GLP-1 receptor agonist developed by Haisco Pharmaceutical. China IND accepted 2023-06-02, approved 2023-08-14. Currently in Phase I for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus. Haisco's second Class 1 innovative drug in diabetes (after HSK7653 DPP-4 inhibitor).
compounds.notes: IND approved 2023-08-14, Phase I ongoing. No PubMed publications yet. Chemical structure not disclosed.

compounds.company: 百极优棠 (广东) 医药科技有限公司 (BPYT / Baiji Youtang)

chemical_identifiers.notes: First-in-class oral small molecule GLP-1 secretagogue (not receptor agonist)

compounds.key_findings: BPYT-01 is a first-in-class oral small molecule GLP-1 secretagogue developed by 百极优棠 (BPYT) with CAS institutes. Unlike GLP-1 receptor agonists, BPYT-01 selectively distributes to intestine and promotes endogenous GLP-1 secretion from L-cells. Discovered from medicinal plant research. China IND approved for T2D (2023-07-18) and Obesity (2024-05-21). Phase I trials actively recruiting. Novel mechanism distinguishes from receptor agonists.
compounds.notes: IND approved for 2 indications (T2D + Obesity). Phase I recruiting. Novel GLP-1 secretagogue mechanism. 5 patents held but numbers not public.

compounds.company: Shanghai Qilu Regor Therapeutics (上海齐鲁锐格医药研发有限公司)

chemical_identifiers.notes: Chemical Drug Class 1 (First-in-class), likely oral small molecule GLP-1 RA

clinical_trials.trial_site: Anhui Medical University Second Hospital, Hefei

compounds.key_findings: RGT-274-CR Capsule is a first-in-class chemical drug from Shanghai Qilu Regor. CDE approved on 2025-05-25 (CXHL2500266) for weight management in adult obesity/overweight. Phase I ongoing as of July 2025 at Anhui Medical University Second Hospital. Company founded 2018, Qilu Pharmaceutical is Series A investor, currently Series B. Related asset RGT-075 (Phase 2 oral GLP-1RA) has Eli Lilly partnership ($50M upfront + $1.5B milestones).
compounds.notes: CDE approved 2025-05-25. Phase I ongoing. Qilu Regor with Eli Lilly partnership on related asset.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Early-stage compound, Roche acquired Carmot for ~$3.1B (2024)

compounds.key_findings: RO7795081/CT-996 is an early-stage compound from Carmot Therapeutics (acquired by Roche in 2024 for ~$3.1B). Not indexed in PubChem or ClinicalTrials.gov as of search date, suggesting preclinical or early Phase I development. CT-996 follows Carmot naming convention (CT-XXX). No publications specifically mention RO7795081 or CT-996. Related compound CT-388 (dual GLP-1/GIP agonist) published in Molecular Metabolism 2026.
compounds.notes: Early-stage Roche/Carmot asset. No public database entries yet. Flag for future monitoring.

chemical_identifiers.notes: First oral GLP-1 receptor agonist, 94% homology to human GLP-1, SNAC absorption enhancer

pubmed_papers.key_2025: 3 major NEJM/Circulation papers on CV outcomes

compounds.key_findings: First oral GLP-1 RA approved by FDA on 2019-09-20. 742+ PubMed publications. HbA1c reduction 0.6-1.4% (dose-dependent). Weight loss up to 15.1% at 50mg (OASIS 1). CV safety established (SOUL 2025). Dosing: 3mg→7mg→14mg once daily on empty stomach. Side effects: nausea, diarrhea, hypoglycemia (>10%); retinopathy monitoring required.
compounds.notes: APPROVED drug with extensive clinical evidence. EMA approved 2020-04-03. China NMPA approved 2020-2021.

compounds.company: Eccogene (诚益生物) → AstraZeneca (阿斯利康)

chemical_identifiers.notes: Oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist, first-in-class

compounds.key_findings: Oral small-molecule GLP-1 RA from Eccogene, licensed to AstraZeneca ($1.825B deal). Phase I completed with 52 participants. Potent GLP-1R binding (IC50 = 2.4 nM). Well tolerated with no SAEs. KEY ADVANTAGE: Once-daily dosing WITHOUT fasting requirements (unlike oral semaglutide). Dose-dependent glucose and weight reductions demonstrated. 9-month NHP study showed NOAEL at 50 mg/kg/day.
compounds.notes: Phase I published 2025. Major differentiator: no fasting required vs Rybelsus.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Long-acting GLP-1 analog, once-weekly subcutaneous

compounds.key_findings: Long-acting GLP-1 analog from Gan & Lee. Once-weekly subcutaneous injection (1-50 μg/kg). Half-life ~7 days in humans. Phase 1 (2025): body weight reduction -1.25 to -1.86 kg (-1.88% to -3.11%) by Day 15. Good tolerability with common AEs being decreased appetite and nausea (mild). No ethnic PK differences between American and Chinese subjects.
compounds.notes: Phase 1 published 2025. Once-weekly dosing. -3.11% weight loss by Day 15.

chemical_identifiers.notes: No public data found. Company established 1988, known for 地奥心血康 capsules.

compounds.key_findings: No public information found for DA-302168S despite extensive search of Chinese pharmaceutical databases (PharmCube, DXY), news sites (Bioon, Sohu), and Baidu Baike. Possible explanations: early-stage compound not yet public, internal development code, different public naming convention, or information behind paywalls. Company (地奥制药) is established 1988 Chengdu-based national high-tech enterprise with 1,780 technical staff.
compounds.notes: NOT FOUND in public sources. Flag for manual verification. May require paid databases (PharmCube, Cortellis) or direct company contact.

chemical_identifiers.notes: Oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist, DISCONTINUED due to liver injury

pubmed_papers.details: 7 metabolites identified via hydroxylation, O-dealkylation, oxetane ring hydrolysis. Lower stability in human/rat liver microsomes vs competitors. Higher potential for DDIs on liver transporters (OATP1B1/OATP1B3). Moderate hepatic metabolism (t1/2 = 208 min in HLM).

compounds.key_findings: Danuglipron (PF-06882961) is an oral small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonist developed by Pfizer for T2D/obesity. DISCONTINUED on 2025-04-14 due to liver injury concerns. 39 PubMed papers on metabolism. Liver concerns: lower stability in liver microsomes, higher DDI potential on OATP1B1/OATP1B3 transporters, moderate hepatic metabolism (t1/2 = 208 min), 7 metabolites identified. News articles about discontinuation removed (404s/blocks). Safety signals likely from internal Pfizer clinical trial data.
compounds.notes: TERMINATED 2025-04-14. Important safety警示 case study. 5 Phase 3 trials terminated.
